name: assisted motion russian twist
instructions: Sit on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Hold the medicine ball with both hands in front of your chest.
Lean back slightly, engaging your abs and keeping your back straight.
Slowly twist your torso to the right, bringing the medicine ball towards the right side of your body.
Pause for a moment, then twist your torso to the left, bringing the medicine ball towards the left side of your body.
Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

Sit on the ground with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Hold the medicine ball with both hands in front of your chest. Lean back slightly, engaging your abs and keeping your back straight. Slowly twist your torso to the right, bringing the medicine ball towards the right side of your body. Pause for a moment, then twist your torso to the left, bringing the medicine ball towards the left side of your body. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
